title: iberis humilis
intro: Iberis humilis, also known as Dwarf Rockcress or Alpine Candytuft, is a low-growing, flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Iberis humilis include its ability to act as an expectorant, relieving congestion and promoting the elimination of mucus, as well as its antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Iberis humilis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
